So you create kind of,

And then some of the lessons we learned from like Augment Code and a few others is like creating these additional sections of the site opens up sometimes a lot of opportunities, especially once you kind of go really heavy towards one and you've done hundreds and hundreds and hundreds of pages, sometimes like it helps.
So for instance, with Augment, we did a slash tools section of the site, but the template is exactly the same, yet like it kind of like really took off, you know?
And so it's like a great, great way to kind of like think about it as just like opening up like additional places of opportunities, you know?
Got it.
Got it.
Great.
Makes sense.
And then the only other thing that we talked about is – Oh, sorry.
Go ahead, Thiago.
Yeah, let's do this.
I think the only thing I didn't see or maybe it was in there is like an uplift on like the design of the site overall.
Yeah.
Not like a complete change, but like, you know, iterate on that and improve.
You know, you had some criticism on this, so I assume you had an opinion.
So, I would love for you guys to take over some of that.
Yeah, that's for sure.
And like just to kind of show you a little bit like with Augment, right?
Like this is not how it looked when we first started, you know?
And so, like, there's a lot of improvements I think we can make on the individual page.
And Augment is a great example of like they started out as like –

You know, dark mode everywhere, but overall, I think there's ways for us to still keep, like, a dark feeling in places, but then the core content, like, improve the legibility and readability of the content itself, you know.
And so we have, like, several, like, kind of examples of that.
And so, anyways, like, that's what we talked about.
gave the team a heads up, and we're pulling in our design and dev to quickly scope it out.

But the main lift here is, which is what I would recommend, is we create the surface area in the fastest way possible to start getting those sections created, right, and the content calibrated, and things kind of start to get indexed.
And then in parallel with the additional work stream, we take over and redo the website outside of Webflow, you know, because then that will really, really add speed.
Like,
I mean, we can spin up sites in, you know, once we agree on, like, the look and feel and design.
And as long as we're not rebranding the company, obviously, right?
Like, it's not a rebrand, it's like a facelift, if you will, an improvement overall to the experience.
Like, we can move really, really, really fast.
And it would be way easier for us to maintain with coding agents and design engineers in the loop than us, you know, dragging and dropping  in Webflow, like we talked about.
So, as long as you're cool with that, like, in parallel, we'll start that work.

But we didn't want to block that way for, you know, defining that work and, you know, and adding a tiny little bit of scope there and going through whatever ProCourse process is from the team just starting to deliver value immediately, you know?
Awesome.
Okay.
So, if you guys can repeat what the, just like summarize, what's the, how much work are you taking off of our place?
You're building, you're basically taking over most of the website.

What we're proposing is taking over and redesigning the – or refreshing the entire website out of Webflow, refactoring the entire website out of Webflow, and in that process, redesigning essentially the pages to be best practices.
Great.
And so, like – and then at that point, you have a repo that anyone else can do changes to, right?
Like, but where we're ideally drawing the line here is that we're not taking on product marketing work, and we're not taking on, like, hey, update our homepage after that.
Like, we're porting everything over to a better place and facelifting the sections of the content that we're going to own.

And then that will set you up for then any dev and any designers, which we can recommend them to, to then, like, spend weeks with you redesigning the homepage if you want, or, you know, creating a more – a cooler animation for your product pages.
Like, that's the work you can't take on, you know, just because it's like
It's a black box, then you never know.
There might be millions of iterations and things like that, and then just a huge suck for us.

But at least we're going to unlock you to, in the future, any design can be implemented way easier, and you can prototype directly in Cursor or whatever you're using, and it just makes it super easy for you to use our components however you want to.
Great.
Awesome.
Okay, it makes a ton of sense.
One of the things we're going to want to do is to connect our product to this experience or, like, embed our product in some degree.
So that's something we are working on that I think will be cool to do here because I saw you have some, you know, some, like, input boxes and so on.

We would like it to be just, like, the Datagrid app at that point that gets embedded, and so it's, like, always the same design system, always the same look, you know, integrating the page, but, like, really the app.
And how we talked about it, Thiago, was we are using your API to programmatically create agents, and agents are, you know, context slash instructions, potentially data sets and tools, right?
And then from there, like, it's essentially almost like a package.

So if someone is in an experience, you are using the API, and then if they click on anything, it essentially will create the environment and use the API to add that agent to their environment, essentially fork that agent, right?
Is that, like, that's how I remember, but I could be wrong because we, you know, we talked about a lot of things.
No, yeah, exactly.
It's like a, it's like a kit, right?

Like, you have, like, knowledge, some attachments, a bunch of instructions, the configuration of the agent, what's on, what's off, the planning instructions, and you can call the API to create that object and push all the associated, you know, data.
Sets and knowledge so that the user can get started with something.
So, like, one example would be, like, a safety agent for California, and it would be, like, OSHA safety standards, right?
And, like, there's a document in there that is, like, the OSHA safety standards that are already, like, PDF attached or whatever.
So, you know, things like this that will kick it off quickly.

Liz, what we could do is, like, the guides or one of the sections can hold a lot of the, quote, unquote, files, know, like, so, for instance, like, a guide to, like, OSHA compliance in California or whatever, right?
Like, whatever safety compliance in California, for example, and that's, like, truly an in-depth guide, and there could even be, like, files from there.
And then the agents will not only relate to that, but can also pull from that.

Maybe, like, we'll think through it, but that way everything, like, it's, like, we're approaching a truly, like, a knowledge graph that we're building that will also accelerate time to value for your actual users, right?
And Tiago said,
So it's not only a way to capture a ton of traffic and AI visibility and get mentioned, but it's also, like, a way for – to accelerate the time to value.
And that's what we're seeing with, like, Lovable, the templates.
Like, they have double-digit conversions, you know, and activations from that because it's just, like – it's what they were thinking of building anyways, you know?
Yeah.
Awesome, Liz.
Amazing work.

Liz and team worked their butts off to, like – I know it's, like, we didn't even cover the whole thing, but it was, like, really, really thoughtful and in-depth, and, you know, and so we – we care deeply, and I told the team, like, Liz is our best, so it's, like, whatever we need to do to knock it out of the park for you all.
Yeah, it looks great.
Awesome.
Cool.
Thanks, team.
We'll follow up soon.
Yeah.
Okay, great.
Thank you.
Bye, y'all.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review GrowthX's proposed website pivot and content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Pivot:** The website will shift from a blog-led awareness channel to a product-led discovery and activation layer, with "Agents" as the central hub.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
  - [**Content Engine:** Content will be built around "Agent-based clusters" to create velocity, moving from keyword-chasing to a product-driven strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [**Website Overhaul:** GrowthX will rebuild the site outside Webflow to enable rapid development and a design refresh, while Datagrid will embed the live app for a seamless user experience.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
  - [**Accelerated TTV:** The site will use the Datagrid API to let users "fork" pre-configured agents directly from the website, dramatically reducing time-to-value (TTV).](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)

## Topics

### Strategic Pivot: Product-Led Discovery

  - [**Goal:** Shift the website from a blog-led awareness channel to a product-led discovery and activation layer.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
  - [**Three Lanes of Expansion:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=228.0)
      - [**Agents:** The canonical discovery layer.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
      - [**Connectors:** A marketplace-style hub for enablement.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)
      - [**Guides & PSEO:** The main traffic engines.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=194.0)

### Content Strategy: Agent-Based Clusters

  - [**Core Principle:** Content clusters will be built around specific agents, moving from keyword-chasing to a product-driven strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=290.0)
  - [**New Site Taxonomy:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [**Agents:** Keep existing URL structure.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [**Connectors:** Keep existing structure, but link directly to the connected integration page (not docs).](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=345.0)
      - [**PSEO (Programmatic SEO):** New pages for specific use cases (e.g., `agents-for-X`).](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=372.0)
      - [**Guides:** A new section for full-funnel informational content.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=403.0)
  - [**Execution Phases:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)
    1.  [**Agents:** Prioritize agents and finalize the landing page UX.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)
    2.  [**Guides & PSEO:** Build out the new sections and PSEO templates.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=521.0)
    3.  [**Connectors:** Expand integration pages with detailed setup content.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=552.0)

### Website Overhaul & Product Integration

  - [**Rationale:** The current Webflow site limits development speed and design flexibility.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Role:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1068.0)
      - [Rebuild the entire website outside Webflow.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=990.0)
      - [Refresh the design and improve content readability.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=911.0)
      - [Create a maintainable code repo for future updates.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1081.0)
  - [**Datagrid's Role:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [Embed the live Datagrid app directly into the website.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [This ensures a consistent design system and provides a seamless user experience.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)

### Accelerating Time-to-Value (TTV)

  - [**Mechanism:** The site will use the Datagrid API to let users "fork" pre-configured agents directly from the website.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1201.0)
  - [**Agent Kits:** These kits include knowledge bases (e.g., OSHA standards), attachments, and instructions.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1244.0)
  - [**Benefit:** This dramatically reduces TTV by providing users with a ready-to-use agent environment, a strategy proven to drive high activation rates (e.g., Lovable templates see double-digit conversions).](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1307.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [Scope the full website rebuild and design refresh.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=980.0)
      - [Prioritize agents to determine the initial content focus.](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=485.0)
  - [**Datagrid:**](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)
      - [Provide the API specifications for embedding the live app and enabling agent "forking."](https://fathom.video/share/WfnFpQMy7esjGBe2NwP6JfNxm-gqFzAC?tab=summary&timestamp=1169.0)

action_items.description: Notify product team re: Notion-to-product sync for Agent library
Prioritize initial Agents w/ Thiago & Kaitlin; then build Agent page
Scope parallel out-of-Webflow rebuild; share design/dev plan w/ Thiago & Kaitlin
Define embed/API integration w/ Thiago; then implement Try this agent CTAs
